title: University Relational Algebra Practice
description: Thirty-seven practice queries that cover the major relational algebra operators on the university schema.

questions.prompt: List the names of all students majoring in Computer Science.

solution.sql: SELECT name FROM student WHERE dept_name = 'Comp. Sci.';

questions.prompt: Find the ids and names of students with at least 90 total credits.

solution.sql: SELECT id, name FROM student WHERE tot_cred >= 90;

questions.prompt: Show the distinct departments represented by the students.

solution.sql: SELECT DISTINCT dept_name FROM student;

questions.prompt: Show the departments that have at least two students.

solution.relational_algebra: π{s1.dept_name}(σ{s1.dept_name = s2.dept_name AND s1.id != s2.id}(ρs1(student) × ρs2(student)))
solution.sql: SELECT DISTINCT s1.dept_name FROM student s1, student s2 WHERE s1.dept_name = s2.dept_name AND s1.id != s2.id;

questions.prompt: List the departments that have either students or instructors.

solution.sql: SELECT dept_name FROM student UNION SELECT dept_name FROM instructor;

questions.prompt: Find departments that currently have courses but no instructors.

solution.sql: SELECT dept_name FROM course EXCEPT SELECT dept_name FROM instructor;

questions.prompt: Return the departments that have at least one student and at least one instructor.

solution.sql: SELECT dept_name FROM student INTERSECT SELECT dept_name FROM instructor;

questions.prompt: Rename the student relation so that id becomes student_id and dept_name becomes major.

solution.sql: SELECT id AS student_id, name, dept_name AS major, tot_cred FROM student;

questions.prompt: List each course along with its title and the budget of the offering department.

solution.sql: SELECT course_id, title, budget FROM course NATURAL JOIN department;

questions.prompt: Find the sections offered in Fall 2017 along with the building, room_number, and classroom capacity.

solution.relational_algebra: pi{course_id, sec_id, semeber, year, building, room_number, capacity}(sigma{semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2017}(section ⋈ classroom))

questions.prompt: Use a Cartesian Product to solve the query: produce student name and course title pairs where the student majors in the same department that offers the course.

solution.relational_algebra: π{student_name, student_major, course_title, course_dept}(ρ{st.name -> student_name, st.dept_name -> student_major, co.title -> course_title, co.dept_name -> course_dept}(σ{st.dept_name = co.dept_name}(ρst(student) × ρco(course))))

questions.prompt: List student names with their corresponding advisor's names.

solution.relational_algebra: pi{student_name, advisor_name}( rho{id->s_id, name->student_name}(student) ⋈ advisor ⋈ rho{id->i_id, name->advisor_name}(instructor))
solution.sql:
SELECT st.name AS student_name, inst.name AS advisor_name
FROM student AS st
JOIN advisor AS adv ON st.id = adv.s_id
JOIN instructor AS inst ON adv.i_id = inst.id;

questions.prompt: Find ids and names of students who have not taken any course.

solution.sql:
SELECT id, name FROM student
EXCEPT
SELECT DISTINCT id, name
FROM student NATURAL JOIN takes;

questions.prompt: Find student-course pairs where the student earned an A or a B.
Return the ids and names of people who appear in both student and instructor tables but have different names.
Show each section together with the meeting day and start time.

solution.relational_algebra: pi{course_id, sec_id, semester, year, day, start_hr, start_min}(section ⋈ time_slot)
solution.sql:
SELECT s.course_id, s.sec_id, s.semester, s.year, t.day, t.start_hr, t.start_min
FROM section AS s NATURAL JOIN time_slot AS t;

questions.prompt: Find the course_ids and section_ids of courses that were offered in Fall 2017 but not in Spring 2018.

solution.relational_algebra: pi{course_id, sec_id}(sigma{semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2017}(section)) - pi{course_id, sec_id}(sigma{semester = 'Spring' AND year = 2018}(section))

questions.prompt: Rename the prerequisite relation so that course_id becomes main_course and prereq_id becomes required_course.

solution.sql: SELECT course_id AS main_course, prereq_id AS required_course FROM prereq;

questions.prompt: Find the ids of students who have taken every Computer Science course (result may be empty).

solution.sql:
SELECT t.id
FROM takes AS t
WHERE t.course_id IN (SELECT course_id FROM course WHERE dept_name = 'Comp. Sci.')
GROUP BY t.id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.course_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM course WHERE dept_name = 'Comp. Sci.');

questions.prompt: List the building, room number and capacity of classrooms with capacity at least 80.

solution.sql: SELECT building, room_number, capacity FROM classroom WHERE capacity >= 80;

questions.prompt: List instructors and their corresponding departments that the instructor's salary is highter than the department's budget.

solution.relational_algebra: π{name, dept_name, salary, budget}(σ{salary > department.budget}(instructor ⋈ department))

questions.prompt: Find the ids and names of students that are not assigned an advisor.

solution.sql:
SELECT st.id, st.name
FROM student AS st
LEFT JOIN advisor AS adv ON st.id = adv.s_id
WHERE adv.s_id IS NULL;

questions.prompt: Retrieve the names of instructors who taught during Spring 2018.
List the courses (id and title) that have no prerequisites.

solution.sql:
SELECT c.course_id, c.title
FROM course AS c
LEFT JOIN prereq AS p ON c.course_id = p.course_id
WHERE p.course_id IS NULL;

questions.prompt: Find student-advisor pairs where both are in the same department.

solution.relational_algebra: pi{student_name, advisor_name}(sigma{student_major = advisor_major}( rho{id->s_id, name->student_name, dept_name->student_major}(student) ⋈ advisor ⋈ rho{id->i_id, name->advisor_name, dept_name->advisor_major}(instructor) ))
solution.sql:
SELECT st.name AS student_name, inst.name AS advisor_name
FROM student AS st
JOIN advisor AS adv ON st.id = adv.s_id
JOIN instructor AS inst ON adv.i_id = inst.id
WHERE st.dept_name = inst.dept_name;

questions.prompt: Return ids (together with couse_ids and grades) of students who have taken the same course more than once.

solution.relational_algebra: π{t1.id, t1.course_id, t1.grade}(σ{t1.id = t2.id and t1.course_id = t2.course_id and (t1.semester != t2.semester or t1.year != t2.year or t1.sec_id != t2.sec_id)}(ρt1(takes) x ρt2(takes)))

questions.prompt: Find departments that currently do not offer any course.
Find all courses taught in the Fall 2017 semester, or in the Spring 2018 semester, or in both.

solution.relational_algebra: pi{course_id}(sigma{semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2017}(teaches)) UNION pi{course_id}(sigma{semester = 'Spring' AND year = 2018}(teaches))

questions.prompt: Find each course title together with the title of its prerequisite course.
Find the names of all instructors in the Physics department, along with the course_id of all courses they have taught.

solution.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT inst.name AS instructor_name, te.course_id
FROM instructor AS inst
JOIN teaches AS te ON inst.id = te.id
WHERE inst.dept_name = 'Physics';

questions.prompt: Find the largest salary value in the university.

solution.sql: SELECT MAX(salary) AS largest_salary FROM instructor;

questions.prompt: Find all courses taught in the Fall 2017 semester and also in the Spring 2018 semester.

solution.relational_algebra: pi{course_id}(sigma{semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2017}(teaches)) INTERSECT pi{course_id}(sigma{semester = 'Spring' AND year = 2018}(teaches))

questions.prompt: Find the names of all instructors in the Comp. Sci. department together with the course titles of all the courses that the instructors teach.

solution.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT inst.name AS instructor_name, c.title AS course_title
FROM instructor AS inst
JOIN teaches AS te ON inst.id = te.id
JOIN course AS c ON te.course_id = c.course_id
WHERE inst.dept_name = 'Comp. Sci.';

questions.prompt: Find the IDs of students who have taken all the courses taught by Instructor 'Crick'.

solution.sql:
SELECT t.id
FROM takes AS t
WHERE t.course_id IN (
  SELECT te.course_id
  FROM teaches AS te
  JOIN instructor AS inst ON te.id = inst.id
  WHERE inst.name = 'Crick'
)
GROUP BY t.id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.course_id) = (
  SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT te.course_id)
  FROM teaches AS te
  JOIN instructor AS inst ON te.id = inst.id
  WHERE inst.name = 'Crick'
);
